name: Rita Fam
translated_text: Food delicious and impressed with the staff service. They go all the way out to help customer needs. A place where I will definitely visit again

name: Flower Soul
translated_text: Food tasted good, menu have alot of varieties that other places dont have, price quite reasonable, staffs also very attentive and friendly

name: FLY4SIX9
translated_text: Taste good & nice service! Will come again

name: S Chin
translated_text: Good food good service

name: Kenny Khoo
translated_text:
Went here to celebrate birthday and their food and service was top notch. The food we ordered came fast even with the high volume of customers they had.

Everything was perfect. The food was delicious and the portion was big, their service was great as they gave high attention to their customers.

For birthdays, you get a selection of their free dish and that too was nice. For each RM50 spent, they will treat you to a free scoop of ice cream of the day. The outlet manager was so nice that he allowed us to trade one ice cream to a matcha ice cream.

They had dining time limit when we went. You can only spend 1.5 hours but that was sufficient for us. Understandable as their table turnover was high.

name: Susan Yong
translated_text: Good food and clean environment. Prompt and polite service from staff.

name: Allan Pang
translated_text: Great place for Japanese food in Melaka.

name: Kai Chee Too
translated_text: The Best Japanese restaurant in Melaka. Served the best sashimi and unique dishes with chefs specialty menu. New dish wagyu skewers are amazing.

name: Vangilyn Lim
translated_text: The food was good, staff were very nice, also a big thanks to the staff named Jamson. He provided excellent service and was especially kind and helpful in assisting our grandfather with his wheelchair in and out. We truly appreciated his care and professionalism. Def will go there again to try other food choices:)

name: DERRYCK KOO
translated_text:
Above average "serve all" type of Japanese restaurant
Excellent service
Good recall on booking made

name: Lina Lim
translated_text:
Hokkaido don is a real disappointment. RM82 is not cheap. But SO MUCH rice and very little sashimi and uni (sea urchin). Sashimi-type of scallop should be sweet tasting but the scallops they have, totally TASTELESS. First time tasting such a tasteless sashimi. No freshness. No taste. Sashimi salmon was average. Uni is little until very pathetic, but taste of it is ok. Just too little for this pricing. Disappointment.

Ordered their nabeyaki udon too. It came with pork shabu which is not bad. Usually it comes chicken but this place dont put chicken in. They put pork shabu slices.

Their yusheng for chinese new year is not bad. Maybe cause the sweet sauce with the yusheng ingredients...

Chawanmushi is pricey too. RM12 for a normal small portion with very usual ingredients inside.

Sanma fish too fishy taste too.

name: eileen Tsao
translated_text: Very nice ambient. Food is fabulous and value for money

name: Wai-Hong Sam
translated_text: Still good for a quick bit of Japanese food

name: Angelababy Fishbaby
translated_text: Svc excellent. Food n beverage 12 likes .. I like their unagi udon. Melting in d mouth. Must try..

name: Jerome DULAS
translated_text:
Very good cuisine with specialties that are not usually found..
Zen atmosphere
Very good service

name: Janey Teh
translated_text: The dining experience was very enjoyable, and a huge part of that was thanks to Hui Xin. I was served by Hui Xin on three separate occasions. Her customer service was absolutely top-notch, and she was able to take my special request orders correctly every time. She also paid great attention to detail and she was very proactive. Will definitely come back in the future.

name: Crystal
translated_text: Super delicious!I love that taco, it’s delicious!And the second glass of wine is half price, highly recommended!

name: sunnymloh
translated_text: Nice authentic Japanese cuisine to dine in Melaka. Food is heavenly & worth the wait. Queue is long like unagi if you don’t book in advance:-)

name: Coyotito D
translated_text:
Ichi Zen in Melaka used to be one of the best Japanese restaurants in town it was once my absolute favourite. Unfortunately, it no longer lives up to its old standards. The quality and experience just aren’t the same anymore.

With so many new and better Japanese spots popping up in Melaka, I find myself choosing other places instead. It’s a little sad to say, because Ichi Zen used to be my go-to. Hoping to see improvements in the future!

name: Eunice Ng Zhi You
translated_text:
increase the price but the portion lesser😑
Although the food taste good but it is a bit expensive

name: Dawn Leow
translated_text:
My monthly must go to restaurant to get my Japanese food fix. Especially the salmon sashimi. As usual the wait staff were efficient and polite. Shout out to Mr Jamson.. job well done. 26/5/2025

Ichi Zen Pavilion KL has always been my favourite place. Today, I decided to try the one in Melaka. It did not disappoint.
I ordered the salmon sashimi set, the chicken teriyaki set and a tempura set. Plus a salmon head syoyaki and black sesame Ebi tempura.

Every dish was as it should be, quality maintained and freshness of the food was great.

I will definitely revisit. Have a look at what we ordered. This place gets a 5☆ on food and the service staff was polite and efficient.
#Japanese #sashimi #Sushi #tempura

1 July 2023 - update,  had the hot pot and it was great. The Macha ice cream was light and not too sweet, as it should be❤️

By
@crazykookycook

name: SYBIL
translated_text:
The food here was great n there were some dishes which I never had B4 at other japanese restaurant..like the tofu in black sesame sauce that was totally delicious 😋 ..n the prawns fried in a black sesame batter..just had to try it n both was totally amazing 😍 ..service crew was on hand all the time very
efficient..atmosphere was ok with some japanese lanterns n a separate sitting at the bar where fresh food is served n cooked..a bit pricey but worth it.. 😋

name: siewfong siewfong
translated_text: The food is delicious and the portions are quite generous. The environment is also very comfortable, and the staff are all very polite. The dishes were served quickly. The sashimi was very fresh and we really liked it.

name: Yoke Fei Phan
translated_text: The location is pretty quiet but once in the dining area, u will see the crowd. Spoilt for choices on menu & would over order due to greed for food!

name: Ding Ling
translated_text:
Ichi Zen isn’t new to me, yet I’ve never thought of leaving a review—until today. Here’s what I ordered:
.
### 🍲 **Sukiyaki Set (RM28)**
The beef slices were decent, the broth was hot, and dipping the meat in cooked it instantly. But here’s the real question—should the raw egg go into the soup or onto the rice? Honestly, I wasn’t sure, but does it really matter? As long as it tastes good, that’s what counts.
.
### 🍊 **Vita C Booster (RM14.80)**
Refreshing, zesty, and left me wanting more. A great pick-me-up.
.
### ☕ **Americano (RM8.80)**
It was okay—nothing to shout about, but then again, this isn’t a specialty coffee house.
.
### 💧 **Drinking Water (RM1)**
A bit surprising to see this on the bill. I personally feel a complimentary glass would be a nice touch, especially when switching between juice and coffee to reset the palate.
.
#### ⭐ **Room for Improvement?**
A few tiny flies were hovering around—not a major issue, but noticeable. Could be due to the lingering seafood aroma in the air. Perhaps better ventilation or pest control measures could help.
.
### **Would I return?**
Yes, whenever I crave a satisfying Japanese meal.
.
### **Would I recommend it?**
Absolutely. The restaurant carries a classic charm, and the ambience makes it a pleasant dining spot.
.
.
JSE@20250331

The only possible complain is that during our visit, it is quite noisy and loud, that is it difficult to talk to the people you came with.

translated_text: The sashimi is fresh and delicious, the ramen is good, and the soup is thick!

name: The Lost Forest Mum
translated_text:
Ichi Zen@Melaka serves high-quality Japanese cuisine, offering a great variety of dishes. During our visit, we tried the Soba Fish Set, Kimchi Soup Duck Ramen, and Pork Broth Shabu Shabu. The Soba Fish Set was well-balanced with fresh fish and perfectly cooked soba noodles. The Kimchi Soup Duck Ramen had a delightful spicy tang, and the duck slices were tender and juicy. The Pork Broth Shabu Shabu was rich and satisfying, with fresh ingredients. Overall, it was an excellent dining experience.

2 🌟 - Below Average: Needs improvement in multiple areas, not meeting expectations.
3 🌟 - Standard, Average: Meets basic expectations, nothing exceptional but acceptable.
4 🌟 - Standard, Above Average: Exceeds expectations slightly, better than the norm.
5 🌟 - Special & Unique: Outstanding experience, highly memorable and exceptional.

name: Johnny Rocket
translated_text:
Excellent Japanese restaurant.
Came here cos I wanted to charge my car while we had lunch.

Had a large family to feed with 3 kids and 5 adults. All in costed RM295. We didn't order sashimi though so that kept the cost down.

The edamame was one of the freshest I've had in Singapore and Malaysia.
Ordered sushi, tamago, sukiyaki, tempura...all were great.

Dessert was very great too. Had moochi and this ice cream redbean pancake.

Highly recommended!

name: Ban Chin
translated_text: This is my first time this restaurant. The food is fast service, but the rice I'm not happy about it. The sushi rice was over sour, and this taste was not a sushi. The sushi It falls apart when you pick it up... The meals in the set menu, the salmon fish don, are not enough shots, and the rice is also fair, too dry... The food is not worth the price... ashame

translated_text: The sushi and sashimi are super fresh, the Gyu Kara Ramen is full of flavor, and the Chashu Curry Yakimeshi is a must try! The restaurant is also very clean and well-maintained, creating a pleasant dining environment. It’s an amazing experience overall, and I’ll definitely be coming back again. Highly recommend!

name: Cairel Ang
translated_text: My 1st dining experience at Ichi Zen Japanese Restaurant ...place is chic and cozy...have the Zen feel upon entering the restaurant. We ordered Grilled Salmon in Creamy Sauce Pasta which was delicious. Then ordered Soba Ramen with Shoyu broth with Tempura Moriawase...portion was big and jumbo shrimps were so fresh and succulent. Also ordered Grilled YakiTori which was tasty...texture of the grilled chicken thigh meats was a "Thumbs-Up" compared to other restaurants we tasted. Lastly we had Agedashi Tofu...yummy. we'll sure dine at Ichi Zen Japanese Restaurant to try other delicious dishes they have to serve.

name: Dr. Alex Quah
translated_text: I think most Malaysian don't know what Japanese food is. Probably because most of them have never get the chance to experience the real thing. The food here is not nice at all, sobered down and not value for money.

translated_text: The sashimi is fresh and the sushi rice is delicious. It can be said to be the best in Malacca👍

name: YongW100
translated_text: The food is nice

name: Luccilla George
translated_text:
Dropped by with my parents to Ichi Zen Restaurant after church today to get a Japanese food fix. It was quite serene inside the restaurant, and very clean.

We ordered 3 main dishes, with 4 pieces of sushi.
1. Gyokai Yakimeshi - Seafood fried rice, it was really good. It has a sufficient amount of seafood pieces like scallops and prawns to compliment the rice. The serving is just nice for 1 person and costed RM14.

2. Tori Momo Esunikku yaki Curry Rice - Rice served with a ladle full of curry with chunks of vegetables, slices of roasted marinated chicken, and a bowl of miso soup. It was a large amount, maybe sufficient for 2 people, it tasted as it should, silky smooth curry gravy which wasn't spicy and a little sweet, and the chicken pieces were juicy. It costed RM29, quite pricey in my opinion.

3. Una Salmon Volcano Don - I think this was one of their speciality, because it was delicious. Sushi rice with cucumber, egg and pickled radish I think in the centre, then topped off with slices of salmon belly and unagi (eel), and a half cooked egg which just binds the whole dish together. It had a few pops that looked like salmon roe, but it had a soy sauce taste in it. Some chemistry action there. Quite pricey this one but worth it - RM35.

4. Four random sushi pieces - Salmon Yuzu Aburi Sushi (RM4), Hana Ebiko Sushi (RM8), Inari Chuka Idako Sushi (RM5), and California Salmon Belly Sushi (RM6). We enjoyed all of them with a good amount of wasabi.

We had hot green tea which was bottomless, to compliment all the dishes.

Overall, we enjoyed our experience there, and surely will visit again soon.

name: Nyen Seng Liew
translated_text: Excellent services by Mr.Jamson who always putting extra effort on assisting us.

name: Jane Yen Shiang Cendol
translated_text:
Overall satisfied with the dining experience.
We are a big group of 15 pax, manage to secure a good seating arrangement via phone reservation. There are more choices because of non halal. Taste and quality are good, and price is reasonable given its good standard. By joining their life-time membership, you may enjoy exclusive dish at very cheap price. Its definitely kids friendly and family friendly place. Service can be a bit slow during peak hour, but still acceptable.
Recommended.
Very satisfied with the dining experience. We were a group of 15 people and we were able to get a good seat after calling.Because of the pork, the menu has more choices.The taste and quality are also good, so the slightly higher price is reasonable.If you become a permanent member, you can enjoy ultra-low discounts on specific dishes, which is worth it.
The service may be a little slow when there are many people, but there is no big problem in being systematic.
Will come back again.

name: JIA TIAN SEAN CHIEW
translated_text: The service is very good and the food is delicious. I hope it will continue 🩷

name: Daniel Teo
translated_text: Walked in at 11 am on a Monday without a reservation and was warmly ushered to a table with four seats. As a solo diner, I appreciated not being shunted to a counter seat for once. Service was efficient, courteous and attentive. The sashimi was very fresh and tasty and excellently presented. This was my most satisfying meal in Melaka and I look forward to an encore visit.

name: Yee Seng Tan

name: Seet Khim

The store feels lively and nice.
The food feels average and expensive considering the price.
There are many restaurants in Melaka where salmon sashimi is more delicious and cheaper.
The tempura udon was a little sweeter than the overseas version, but it was heartwarming.
The attention to detail from the staff was very good.
I wonder if there will be a repeat?

name: Robin Lau
translated_text: Melaka has wazen, ichizen and senjutei, which are the holy trinity of japanese restaurants. These types of japanese restaurant you should know what you will be paying. So price aside, i think they have their 'specialty' food which has the truffle sauce thing. You can try it in the sushi/alacartes/noodle. I would highly recommend to try some of that and others are all on standard. Disappointing food for me is the katsu mentai don iirc, not good. The serving time is relatively fast so a big plus for me. Use public bank card got discount. Recommend to try this place at least once or twice.

name: 韓晓茂

The best service in the restaurant goes to the person who served tea.  Gently and humbly served frequently according to need.

name: Jael Teng
translated_text: Sashimi was fresh and was thick cut. The ramen (pork) broth was thick, noodles did not have alkaline taste. Green tea was topped up often without need to constantly request. Service depended on your server. I felt our server was okay, but there were others that appeared unbothered.

translated_text: Good quality and fresh japanese food unlike what you find inside shopping mall. Their portion is definitely worth it. Lastly, the waiters are very professional with good manner. Well done!

name: Alan Chan
translated_text: This is one of the Japanese restaurants in Malacca that I would recommend.The food is delicious, the price is moderate, the environment and service are good, and the location is easy to find.

name: Yoong Kee Gin

Recommend their Sashimi and buta kakuni (always soft and succulent)...make an early booking just to be safe. Definitely worth a try or 2.

translated_text: Food quality level of Melaka superb and value for money. Photo attached.

Was running out of options on a Sunday night so just picked the restaurant based on sight while driving. Could have asked us to wait for 10mins for a proper table as we had children, I stead of pushing counter seat to us.
Stone rice was great, the rest was ok. Price is on the high side for a chain restaurant. Overall still recommended.

name: Chow Hon Keong
translated_text:
This is a Japanese restaurant located Syahbandar area of Melaka.

It is a fairly large restaurant.  The interior setup is nicely done.  A nice atmosphere to have a nice Japanese meal.

The food preparation standard here is good.  Food especially the Sashimi are fresh.  The menu is reasonably wide.  Food presentations are good too.

Pork dishes are served here too.

Food portions are acceptable and ideal.

The service is good, fast and professional.

Cleanliness is good.

Public washrooms are available within the restaurant.

Prices are reasonable.  Cash, e-wallet and credit card payments are accepted here.

Parkings are roadside parkings.  There are numerous shops, restaurants and offices nearby, as a result; during peak periods, getting a parking lot may require some patience.

Handicapped patrons can patronize this restaurant easily.

11-06-2023 : The Udon and Fried Fish were fantastic.  Both are recommended.

name: 郑邱铭
translated_text: Very good service attitude

name: BL Nai
translated_text:
Been to this restaurant few times and the food standards still the best and maintained well. The presentation good and authentic. The Prawn Tempura nice & crispy.
Service can be improved with a smile as all staff seemed to be rushing.
Will definitely dine again in the near future.

name: H.Sakinah
translated_text:
Not so hidden spot when it comes to authentic japanese cuisine in Melaka. The place offers wide range of menu selection according to your likings.

The sashimi is fresh and doesn't have the fishy smells at all. Definitely feel the satisfaction when ordering the lunch set that came with rice, chicken thigh and miso soup.

Ordered a set of Gyoza and surprisingly it taste exactly how it looks. Crispy on the outside and the fillings, chef kiss!

**Easy to park as there's alot of parking space
**Mind their operation hours and call to reserve first to avoid disappointment

name: KENNN H
translated_text: The service attitude is very good, the sashimi is fresh and has no odor, and the wasabi quality is also very good. However, the size of the sushi varies from big to small, and the price is on the high side.

name: Chuck Phoon
translated_text:
Bad management ignore for change table, mention the table is for 5 people but end up sitting is 3 person and 4 person. Management and staff completely ignore the door wind blow in blame to heavy rain outside (bad weather). Is
Good you can feel like dine outdoor with rain water and window blow into you 👍🏻. Pay one price can enjoy 2 environment

name: Kenji
translated_text:
I stayed here for 2 months on a business trip and learned about this place.
All dishes are of high quality.Sushi is not much different from Japan.Salmon in particular is cost-effective and delicious.The stir-fried vegetables were also delicious.The hot tea was served and it felt like being in Japan.
The atmosphere of the store is bright and nice.There is a restaurant called Fugo Ryori in Malacca, but I think it is inferior overall to a single meal.The customer service of the store staff was very poor.

name: Annie Yong
translated_text: Tip top services by Jamson. Friendly, informative and assertive. Nice food . Will come back again.

name: Delesh Avalani
translated_text: Loved it because they had a special vegetarian menu. The food was excellent and the service was great well. Do book in advance.

name: deTraveller
translated_text: Another Good Japanese Restaurant. Try the Kimchi, Hamburger, Sesame Tempura… Best

name: HS Tew
translated_text: The foods are nice.

name: Tastan Qiong
translated_text: Fresh foods, nice taste, clean environment & classic design... price tag may a bit high but is worth it without value.

name: Jyotshna Bhandari
translated_text: Very delicious food.

name: Mark Yeo
translated_text:
We are back in melaka after 3 years and visited this place for dinner on Sat evenin. Walled in around 615pm and settled comfortably at counter since its only 2 of us. Most of the table seats had reservations.

The menu was extensive and easy to read. We placed our order with the wait staff by reference to the letters and numbers tag to the item. We ate the following:
Tuna sashimi
Baked oysters in cheese and mushroom
Shishamo grilled
Clams in garlic butter gravy
4 pcs salmon roll in truffle flavour dressing
Grilled beef with inoki mushrooms

The tuna sashimi was fresh and the slices were thick. Everything else (cooked items) we ordered tasted good. The service staff were all friendly and helpful, answering our queries patiently.  We had an enjoyable dinner and hope to be back the next time we are in melaka.

Adding pics from our second visit in March 2023.

name: erzz
translated_text: I always come back for Japanese food here with family. Fresh and delicious And The Decorations is nice too. Staff very friendly when I ask for extra tableware. 😊

The egg roll was a refrigerated ready-to-eat food that tasted the same as the cheap egg sushi in Aeon but with some mayo on it. The price is still very expensive. You can buy a big bag outside. The dessert mochi is ordinary glutinous rice balls made into squares and baked for you. It costs more than 10 yuan. Don't go to avoid lightning!

a bit pricey

name: L Wl
translated_text: A Japanese restaurant who ran out of sashimi, crabs amounting to around 50% of their menus. Should have preempted us when we called in to book instead of only letting us know when we tried to order..

name: Ee Wen Chiang
translated_text:
My family and relatives will definitely select ichizen as a restaurant for family gathering or celebration over 8 or 9 years. Not only fresh, mouthwatering dishes with premium quality and free flow of green tea, the interior design is impeccably luxurious and surrounded by tranquil Japanese instrumental music.  As a member, we always enjoy the benefit of 10% discount.

Surprisingly, the smoothies such as avocado kiwi, summer berries and peach milk are smooth and refreshing like basking in the breezy wind.

For constructive feedback, the portion like salmon and eel has been reduced compared to past, so it is overpriced.

name: Jane Ng
translated_text:
One of the best Japanese restaurants in the city of Melaka. There are wide selection of food from raw to cooked food.
The restaurant is always crowded especially during the weekends and public holidays. Therefore l would strongly recommend that you make reservations before you come.
The service is good and pretty fast. And most importantly the food is fresh and tasty.
Public bank debit/credit card holders will get 10% on total bill.

name: Hin Wai Chang
translated_text: Located in the gentrified enclave of Melaka, this place is quintessential  good Japanese food. With the impeccable freshness of the sashimi, heartwarming nabemono, and quaint Japanese fried, the copious options available is surprising. Unfortunately, I couldn't post more photos. The picture-taking simply can't keep up with eating.

name: Leonard Tan
translated_text: Food was good and value for money

name: Angela Liu
translated_text:
The salmon in the rice bowl is cut very thin
Not worth it
Chawanmushi is not delicious and there is no ginkgo in it.

The service was okay, but they were not very proactive in helping me add tea.
And I have to collect the plates before I finish eating.
Feeling very bad

This kind of store doesn’t have wifi
I don’t even need a wet wipe.

Cp value is very low

translated_text: food is delicious! great service and very clean. only downside is that food is a bit expensive.

translated_text: Food is getting smaller and smaller, the price has gone up but the quality has gone down 🙂

translated_text: Food is great! Service is even better!! Just feel relaxed to dine here. 5 stars!

name: MARVIS KHO
translated_text: Wide selection of food. Fresh and yummy. Service was quick. Staff were friendly. Plenty of parking lots outside. Restaurant is clean and comfortable.
